perturbation.original_text: 9. NO WARRANTIES. EACH PARTY PROVIDES ALL MATERIALS AND SERVICES TO THE OTHER PARTY "AS IS." EACH PARTY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. Each party acknowledges that it has not entered into this Agreement in reliance upon any warranty or representation except those specifically set forth herein. Unless an approval process is specified herein, all deliverables provided by one party to the other shall be deemed accepted (for purposes of the UCC) when delivered.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. NO WARRANTIES. EACH PARTY PROVIDES ALL MATERIALS AND SERVICES TO THE OTHER PARTY "AS IS," except for theglobe, who warrants that all materials and services are of the highest quality and suitable for their intended purpose. EACH PARTY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. Each party acknowledges that it has not entered into this Agreement in reliance upon any warranty or representation except those specifically set forth herein. Unless an approval process is specified herein, all deliverables provided by one party to the other shall be deemed accepted (for purposes of the UCC) when delivered.
perturbation.explanation: The original section disclaims all warranties. The change introduces an exception, stating that theglobe warrants high quality and suitability, directly contradicting the initial disclaimer. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the warranties provided under the agreement and could lead to disputes over the quality of theglobe's materials and services. Because the globe provides all their material to Boxlot, Boxlot can dispute the services based on the change. The globe can argue with the rest of the text disclaiming all warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. NO WARRANTIES. EACH PARTY PROVIDES ALL MATERIALS AND SERVICES TO THE OTHER PARTY "AS IS," except for theglobe, who warrants that all materials and services are of the highest quality and suitable for their intended purpose. EACH PARTY DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. Each party acknowledges that it has not entered into this Agreement in reliance upon any warranty or representation except those specifically set forth herein. Unless an approval process is specified herein, all deliverables provided by one party to the other shall be deemed accepted (for purposes of the UCC) when delivered.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims all warranties. The changed text introduces an exception specifically for 'theglobe,' stating they warrant the highest quality and suitability. This directly contradicts the general disclaimer in the same section. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, highlighting the self-contradictory nature of the clause. This creates an in-text contradiction, making the enforceability of warranty claims against 'theglobe' ambiguous, because the same section disclaims all warranties, but offers one for the globe. The explanation notes that Boxlot can dispute the services based on the change. The globe can argue with the rest of the text disclaiming all warranties. The change directly conflicts with the original intention of the clause, and falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
